realtimeInsights.title: 2026-05-27 Caulfield Heath RR7. Tile Importer Handicap 1. Call To Glory

realtimeInsightAcf.description: Caulfield R7 no. 1- CALL TO GLORY looked like he needed the run first up run over 1000m in Benchmark 84 company at Caulfield where he led on a modest early tempo before struggling to quicken late. Expecting improvement second up in weaker grade.

realtimeInsightAnalyst.name: James Molony
realtimeInsightAnalyst.description: James has been involved in the racing industry for over 17 years, from humble beginnings as a bagman on the rails to now turned full time punter for the last six years. James continually looks to enhance his market edge with the use of video analysis in combination with a data driven approach as he tries to map out how a race will be run. James predominantly bets in NSW and Victoria but is always on the lookout for value across all states.

realtimeInsightAcf.description: Canterbury R5 no. 3- SKYCATCHER deserves another chance second up after getting caught back on the rail over 1350m on resumption at Wyong last start.

realtimeInsightAnalyst.name: James Molony
realtimeInsightAnalyst.description: James has been involved in the racing industry for over 17 years, from humble beginnings as a bagman on the rails to now turned full time punter for the last six years. James continually looks to enhance his market edge with the use of video analysis in combination with a data driven approach as he tries to map out how a race will be run. James predominantly bets in NSW and Victoria but is always on the lookout for value across all states.

realtimeInsights.title: 2026-05-27 Canterbury RR4. Tab Handicap 4. Sapphire Rose

realtimeInsightAcf.description: Canterbury R4 no. 4- SAPPHIRE ROSE looks among the early speed influences in the race and should relish the heavy track having won over 1200m on a heavy 8 at Canterbury last campaign.

realtimeInsightAnalyst.name: James Molony
realtimeInsightAnalyst.description: James has been involved in the racing industry for over 17 years, from humble beginnings as a bagman on the rails to now turned full time punter for the last six years. James continually looks to enhance his market edge with the use of video analysis in combination with a data driven approach as he tries to map out how a race will be run. James predominantly bets in NSW and Victoria but is always on the lookout for value across all states.

realtimeInsights.title: 2026-05-27 Canterbury RR3. Asahi Super Dry Handicap 4. The Iron Star

realtimeInsightAcf.description: Canterbury THE IRON STAR (Race 3 No. 4) Narrowly beaten at Hawkesbury last start however produced the best last start rating in this field and Tyler Schiller retains the ride. Can mix form however the odds good enough to find out if the four-year-old is up to the task.

realtimeInsightAnalyst.name: Greg Polson
realtimeInsightAnalyst.description:
Greg Polson has been making a living from horse racing for close to 20 years in a variety of roles including broadcasting on RSN radio, selling speed maps and tipping professionally.

Data is central to Greg’s form analysis using speed ratings as the starting point and adjusting for factors such as speed maps, gear changes etc. to arrive at his own price rating. Greg follows the value within a race and is happy betting on multiple horses within a race provided he sees the value in the selections. Greg will bet predominantly in NSW but will branch out to other states during key carnival times.

realtimeInsights.title: 2026-05-27 Caulfield Heath RR4. Ive > Handicap 3. Golden Magnate

realtimeInsightAcf.description: Caulfield R4 no. 3- GOLDEN MAGNATE looks to have plenty of upside after clocking fast early splits leading on debut over 1214m at Moe before holding out the late challenge of Miss Lola to score.

realtimeInsightAnalyst.name: James Molony
realtimeInsightAnalyst.description: James has been involved in the racing industry for over 17 years, from humble beginnings as a bagman on the rails to now turned full time punter for the last six years. James continually looks to enhance his market edge with the use of video analysis in combination with a data driven approach as he tries to map out how a race will be run. James predominantly bets in NSW and Victoria but is always on the lookout for value across all states.

realtimeInsights.title: 2026-05-27 Canterbury RR2. Toyota Forklifts Handicap 1. Snitzel Miss

realtimeInsightAcf.description: Canterbury SNITZEL MISS (Race 2 No. 1) Should have finished closer to the winner at Kensington resuming when fourth beaten just over two-lengths after being help up early in the straight. Ratings profile suggests there is about four-lengths improvement to come and every reason to believe the filly can get close to that number with even luck and the first up run under her belt.

realtimeInsightAnalyst.name: Greg Polson
realtimeInsightAnalyst.description:
Greg Polson has been making a living from horse racing for close to 20 years in a variety of roles including broadcasting on RSN radio, selling speed maps and tipping professionally.

Data is central to Greg’s form analysis using speed ratings as the starting point and adjusting for factors such as speed maps, gear changes etc. to arrive at his own price rating. Greg follows the value within a race and is happy betting on multiple horses within a race provided he sees the value in the selections. Greg will bet predominantly in NSW but will branch out to other states during key carnival times.

realtimeInsights.title: 2026-05-27 Caulfield Heath RR3. Sportsbet Jockey Watch Handicap 3. Powerful Torque

realtimeInsightAcf.description: Caulfield R3 no. 3- POWERFUL TORQUE looks suited dropping back in trip with improvement out of a fast Benchmark 78 over 2000m at Caulfield where he went too quick leading early before tiring late.

realtimeInsightAnalyst.name: James Molony
realtimeInsightAnalyst.description: James has been involved in the racing industry for over 17 years, from humble beginnings as a bagman on the rails to now turned full time punter for the last six years. James continually looks to enhance his market edge with the use of video analysis in combination with a data driven approach as he tries to map out how a race will be run. James predominantly bets in NSW and Victoria but is always on the lookout for value across all states.

realtimeInsights.title: 2026-05-27 Canterbury RR1. Exceedance @ Vinery Stud Handicap 1. Celestial Charm

realtimeInsightAcf.description: Canterbury CELESTIAL CHARM (Race 1 No. 1) On debut and has won two of four trials including the latest at Randwick where the filly settling just off the speed. The time was there and Tommy Berry stays in the saddle after being on board in the trial.

realtimeInsightsNextToGo.title: 2026-05-27 Caulfield Heath RR7. Tile Importer Handicap 1. Call To Glory

realtimeInsightAcf.description: Caulfield R7 no. 1- CALL TO GLORY looked like he needed the run first up run over 1000m in Benchmark 84 company at Caulfield where he led on a modest early tempo before struggling to quicken late. Expecting improvement second up in weaker grade.

realtimeInsightsNextToGo.title: 2026-05-27 Canterbury RR4. Tab Handicap 4. Sapphire Rose

realtimeInsightAcf.description: Canterbury R4 no. 4- SAPPHIRE ROSE looks among the early speed influences in the race and should relish the heavy track having won over 1200m on a heavy 8 at Canterbury last campaign.

realtimeInsightsNextToGo.title: 2026-05-27 Canterbury RR3. Asahi Super Dry Handicap 4. The Iron Star

realtimeInsightAcf.description: Canterbury THE IRON STAR (Race 3 No. 4) Narrowly beaten at Hawkesbury last start however produced the best last start rating in this field and Tyler Schiller retains the ride. Can mix form however the odds good enough to find out if the four-year-old is up to the task.
